title: API announces 47th edition of foundational Line Pipe standard - World Pipelines
summary: The American Petroleum Institute has published the 47th edition of API Specification 5L for line pipe. This foundational standard outlines requirements for the safe manufacture of seamless and welded steel pipe used to transport oil, gas, and other energy products. The latest edition introduces critical requirements across more than 15 topic areas. Key updates focus on high-frequency weld pipe quality and requirements for pipelines transporting carbon dioxide. Technical revisions address hydrotest pressure gauge calibration, non-destructive evaluation personnel certification, and magnetic particle testing. The standard also updates guidelines for tensile test flattening, record retention, and sour-service performance. Additional changes include chemical element ranges, impact toughness requirements, lamination control, and sulfide stress cracking test requirements. Manufacturers capable of meeting these updated criteria can apply for the voluntary API Monogram licensing program. The new standard incorporates technical feedback and insights from subject matter experts representing more than a dozen countries.

title: United Arab Emirates CCI Piping Systems - Market Analysis, Forecast, Size, Trends and Insights - IndexBox
summary: The United Arab Emirates CCI piping systems market is highly import-dependent, with over 80 percent of its annual demand met by foreign suppliers due to a lack of domestic manufacturing for core components. High-purity semiconductor and electronics cleanrooms constitute the largest demand segment at 35 to 45 percent, followed by industrial automation and instrumentation at 25 to 30 percent. The market is projected to expand at a compound annual rate of 5.5 to 7 percent from 2026 to 2035, driven by manufacturing capacity expansions in Abu Dhabi and Dubai. Premium-grade ultra-high-purity systems are capturing an increasing share of procurement value, rising to 25 to 30 percent as semiconductor specifications tighten. Domestic value-add is restricted to secondary operations like cutting, orbital welding, pre-assembly, and validation services conducted in logistics hubs like Jebel Ali. Supply chains face bottlenecks from lengthy material qualification approvals and 8 to 12 percent annual price swings for critical alloys and thermoplastics. International production constraints have periodically extended lead times past 20 weeks, prompting local distributors to increase safety stock by 15 to 20 percent. International manufacturers dominate the competitive landscape through regional distributors, with the top five suppliers controlling 55 to 65 percent of market revenue. The country also functions as a regional redistribution hub, re-exporting 15 to 20 percent of imported piping volume to other Middle Eastern and African markets.

title: Integration & Turnkey in Tubes & Pipes: From Manufacturing Control to Project-Level Execution - Tube & Pipe India
summary: Integration in the Indian tube and pipe industry is not a universal default but is adopted selectively based on project-specific requirements. This integration manifests in various configurations, including raw material linkage, internal manufacturing control, downstream processing, and system-level delivery. Companies like Maharashtra Seamless integrate steelmaking with pipe manufacturing to control material properties and application consistency. Manufacturing-level integration focuses primarily on internal process control and product compliance to meet international standards. Conversely, system-level integration expands offerings to include associated components like coatings, fittings, and valves to reduce interface risks. Buyers in project-driven environments, such as oil and gas or water infrastructure, drive the demand for these consolidated systems. Turnkey execution represents a deeper layer of integration where manufacturers align directly with contractors to manage project delivery and specifications. This execution-level capability mitigates installation risks and ensures compatibility across the installed infrastructure. Standalone manufacturing remains highly viable for industry segments with standardized and modular product requirements.

title: PHMSA Issues Multiple Rulemakings to Update, Clarify, or Amend the Federal Pipeline Safety Regulations and to Incorporate Updated Industry Standards - The National Law Review
summary: On April 24, 2026, the Pipeline and Hazardous Materials Safety Administration issued 40 regulatory updates amending parts 191, 192, and 195 of the federal pipeline safety regulations. These rulemakings affect operators of gas, hazardous liquid, carbon dioxide, underground natural gas storage, and liquefied natural gas facilities. Public comments are due by June 23, 2026, with most final rules taking effect on August 3, 2026. Proposed changes include extending annual report filing deadlines and raising the monetary thresholds that trigger construction notifications. The proposals also eliminate outdated welding limitations and extend mainline valve inspection intervals for hazardous liquid pipelines to a maximum of 15 months. Operators would be permitted to use remote sensing technologies for right-of-way patrols and to remotely monitor rectifiers. Additionally, the proposals raise the property damage threshold for reporting releases and eliminate redundant material verification during pressure reconfirmations. The operational timeframe for newly installed rupture-mitigation valves would increase from 14 to 90 days. Direct final rules clarify hazardous liquid pipeline integrity management, allow electronic response plan storage, and incorporate 15 updated industry standards. Any direct final rules receiving adverse comments by the deadline will be withdrawn and republished as notices of proposed rulemakings.

title: J.F. Lehman’s Forged Solutions Acquires Custom Alloy - Private Equity Professional
summary: J.F. Lehman & Company portfolio company Forged Solutions Group has acquired Custom Alloy Corporation. This acquisition realigns Custom Alloy within the J.F. Lehman portfolio following its 2023 acquisition by Trident Maritime Systems during a bankruptcy process. Forged Solutions Group specializes in manufacturing high-specification forged metal components, including rolled ring, closed-die, open-die, and extrusion forgings, for aerospace, defense, and space applications. Custom Alloy is a vertically integrated manufacturer producing open- and closed-die forgings along with seamless and welded pipe fittings for high-pressure, corrosive, and nuclear environments. Custom Alloy holds Level 1 qualifications from the U.S. Navy as both a forging and fitting manufacturer, making it one of only three national companies to hold both designations. The company serves as the sole-source manufacturer for Columbia-class Main Seawater Fittings and a dual-source supplier for Virginia-class Main Seawater Fittings. The acquisition provides Forged Solutions Group with critical exposure to the U.S. nuclear navy supply chain. This transaction positions the combined entity to address bottlenecks in the shipbuilding supply chain and meet demand driven by U.S. Navy submarine programs and the AUKUS partnership. Custom Alloy operates four manufacturing facilities totaling 450,000 square feet near Allentown in High Bridge, New Jersey.
